title: diplolepis australis
intro:
Diplolepis australis, also known as the New Zealand mistletoe or kawakawa mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species native to New Zealand that grows on the branches of the kawakawa tree (Macropiper excelsum).

This plant is traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal properties, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Diplolepis australis include potential relief from pain, fever reduction, and antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent, allowing the medicinal compounds to be extracted and utilized for their health benefits.
